feature_name: Azure AD Authentication Required for Data Plane Access
notes: Microsoft Entra ID authentication GA Nov 2024 for Basic/Standard/Premium tiers (not Enterprise tiers). Disabling access keys and enforcing Entra auth is configurable. Azure Policy built-in: 'Azure Cache for Redis should not use access keys for authentication'. ARM check: properties.disableAccessKeyAuthentication.

feature_name: Microsoft Defender for Cloud - Adaptive Application Controls
notes: feature_supported=Not Applicable; Adaptive Application Controls requires VM agents; Azure Cache for Redis is PaaS.

feature_name: Service Credential and Secrets Support Integration and Storage in Azure Key Vault
notes: feature_supported=False; Redis does not store operational secrets in Key Vault; PSK/access keys managed via Azure portal/CLI.

Azure Cache for Redis is fully managed PaaS — no customer-accessible OS layer, no VM, no container image. Anti-malware (Defender for Endpoint, Windows Defender, ClamAV) cannot be deployed by customers. Microsoft manages the underlying OS internally. Structurally not applicable for any Redis SKU.
feature_supported=Not Applicable; Anti-malware health monitoring requires compute agent; Redis is PaaS.
feature_supported=Not Applicable; Azure Automation DSC requires OS agent; Redis is PaaS.

Azure Automation Update Management patches OS-level software on VMs. Azure Cache for Redis is fully managed PaaS — Microsoft patches the underlying OS. Customers have no OS access. This control is structurally inapplicable for all Redis SKUs.
feature_supported=False; Azure Backup not supported for Azure Cache for Redis; no backup service integration.
feature_supported=Not Applicable; Guest Configuration requires compute agent; Redis is PaaS.
LIVE-DIRECT: Azure Policy built-in definitions available for Azure Cache for Redis compliance.

Entra ID RBAC via ACL-based access policies GA Nov 2024 for Basic/Standard/Premium tiers. Three built-in policies: Data Owner, Data Contributor, Data Reader. Custom policies supported. ARM resources: Microsoft.Cache/redis/accessPolicies and Microsoft.Cache/redis/accessPolicyAssignments. Check disableAccessKeyAuthentication + list access policy assignments.

feature_name: Certificate Management in Azure Key Vault
notes: Azure Cache for Redis manages its own TLS certificates internally (Microsoft-managed). Customers cannot supply custom TLS certificates via Azure Key Vault for the Redis endpoint. Only minimumTlsVersion is customer-configurable (1.2 enforced from Apr 2025). KV integration exists only for CMK disk encryption keys (Enterprise tier, DP-5/DP-6), not for TLS certificate management.

Since Entra ID auth is GA for Redis (Nov 2024), Azure AD Conditional Access policies do apply to the Entra authentication flow for Redis data plane access. However CA policies are configured in Entra ID / Microsoft Graph — not in the Redis ARM resource. Not checkable via azure-mgmt-redis SDK. Requires IM-1 (Entra auth) to be enabled first. Script not feasible via Redis ARM alone.

Official Customer Lockbox supported services list (as of 2025-05) does NOT include Azure Cache for Redis. List includes Azure SQL, Azure Storage, AKS, Azure Functions, etc. — but not Redis. Status unchanged from v3 baseline.
feature_supported=Not Applicable; Custom container images require ACI/AKS; Redis is PaaS.
feature_supported=Not Applicable; Custom VM images require IaaS; Redis is PaaS.

feature_name: Data at Rest Encryption Using CMK
notes: CMK disk encryption GA Feb 2024 for Enterprise and Enterprise Flash tiers ONLY. Basic/Standard/Premium use Microsoft-managed keys only; CMK not supported on those tiers. Enterprise check via Microsoft.Cache/redisEnterprise resource. If org uses only Basic/Standard/Premium, verdict is still_not_applicable.

feature_name: Data at Rest Encryption Using Platform Keys
notes: microsoft_managed: Azure Cache for Redis encrypts all data at rest using platform-managed keys by default.
microsoft_managed: Azure Cache for Redis enforces TLS 1.2 for all data in transit by default.
feature_supported=False; No native DLP for Redis; cache is transient in-memory store, not suitable for DLP controls.

feature_name: Microsoft Defender for Service / Product Offering
notes: Defender for Cloud support matrix (2024): Azure Cache for Redis receives only CSPM recommendations (foundational), no security alerts, no vulnerability assessment. Defender for Open-Source Relational Databases covers PostgreSQL/MySQL/MariaDB only — Redis is NOT a relational database and is NOT included. No Defender for Redis product exists. v3 baseline status unchanged: False / Not Applicable.
microsoft_managed: Azure Cache for Redis enforces public network access disabled by default when Private Link configured; publicNetworkAccess property.
feature_supported=Not Applicable; EDR requires compute agent; Redis is PaaS.

feature_name: Key Management in Azure Key Vault
notes: Azure Key Vault integration for key management (CMK) GA Feb 2024 for Enterprise and Enterprise Flash tiers. Enterprise tier uses KV to store key encryption keys (KEK) for disk encryption. Basic/Standard/Premium: no KV key management integration. Same tier restriction as DP-5.
feature_supported=False; Azure Cache for Redis is a fully managed PaaS; no local admin accounts concept.

feature_name: Local Authentication Methods for Data Plane Access
notes: microsoft_managed: Azure Cache for Redis uses AAD-based access keys as local auth mechanism; TLS enforced by default.

Client applications can authenticate to Redis data plane using managed identity tokens via Entra ID (GA Nov 2024). Additionally, cache-side managed identity for storage connections (persistence/import-export) GA for Premium tier. Check: Entra auth enabled + access policy assignments with MI object IDs. Entra auth required first (IM-1).
feature_supported=False; No native backup capability; Azure Cache for Redis does not support Azure Backup service.
LIVE-DIRECT: Azure Cache for Redis (Premium/Enterprise) supports VNet injection with NSG on the delegated subnet.
LIVE-DIRECT: Azure Cache for Redis (Premium/Enterprise) supports Private Endpoint for private connectivity.
LIVE-DIRECT: Azure Cache for Redis supports diagnostic logs (connection events, cache activity) via Azure Monitor.

Microsoft Purview data map cannot scan Azure Cache for Redis. Purview scans structured/semi-structured stores (Azure SQL, Blob Storage, ADLS Gen2, Cosmos DB). Redis is an in-memory key-value cache — data is ephemeral, schema-less, and not scannable by Purview. No native data classification feature exists for Redis. Purview can detect Redis connection strings as secrets (Defender CSPM secrets scanning) but that is not data classification.

Service principals can authenticate to Redis data plane via Entra ID tokens (GA Nov 2024). Same mechanism as managed identities — service principal object ID assigned to access policy. Check: Entra auth enabled + access policy assignments include SP-type objectIds. Entra auth required first (IM-1).
LIVE-DIRECT: Azure Cache for Redis (Premium/Enterprise) supports VNet injection for network isolation.

Defender for Cloud support matrix: Azure Cache for Redis receives CSPM recommendations only — no security alerts column, no vulnerability assessment column. Defender for Open-Source Relational Databases (covers PostgreSQL/MySQL/MariaDB) does NOT include Redis. No Defender VA scan product exists for Redis.
